actions.text: approach the front door and try the handle
step into the entrance hall and let your eyes adjust to the dark
stand still and listen carefully for any sound in the house
Robert? It's Edmund. If you can hear me, call out.
light your lantern and move toward the staircase
The candle in the upstairs window has moved — it now burns in a different room than before
check the ground floor rooms before committing to the staircase
push open the parlour door and raise the lantern
Something drips steadily in a room where there is no plumbing
move toward the kitchen, where the dripping seems to come from
examine the cellar door off the kitchen
This lock is new. Robert would have had the key. Who replaced it, and why?
examine the lock closely for tool marks or signs of forced entry
climb the staircase, keeping close to the wall to avoid creaking boards
A shadow moves under the door of the upstairs study — but you heard no footsteps on the landing
press your back to the wall outside the study door and wait
push the study door open slowly and hold the lantern high
move to Robert's desk and read the topmost page carefully
The geometry of this place. Robert wrote that phrase three times. What did you mean, Robert?
Something brushes against the outside of the study window — on the third floor, with nothing below it
examine the diagram pinned to the wall above the desk
follow the diagram's marked route to a specific point in the room
The room feels smaller than when you entered. The corners do not meet at right angles.
check the floorboards under the desk for anything unusual
There's a panel here. Someone built this into the floor deliberately and recently.
lift the panel and lower your lantern into the space below
The candle on the desk extinguishes on its own, leaving only your lantern
descend the hidden stair below the panel, counting each step
move carefully along the passage below, keeping one hand on the wall
The passage ends at a door that leads into the cellar — standing open, unlocked from the inside
